html:
RECOUP Hypermobility Rehabilitation Program

Introduction

Ehlers-Danlos Syndromes (EDS) and Hypermobility Spectrum Disorders (HSD) are a group of heritable connective tissue disorders. Either directly or indirectly, EDS is known or thought to alter the biology of collagen in the body (the most abundant protein), which can lead to multi-systemic symptoms. Each type has distinct physical traits, and, with the notable exception of the most common form, the hypermobile type of EDS, most types are associated with a known disease-causing gene. There are physical characteristics that are common to all types of EDS, including hypermobile joints (joints that move in greater amounts than expected) and skin involvement, such as any of the following: soft, stretchy, saggy, too thin, easy bruising, easy wounding, poor wound healing, and/or atrophic scarring. The prevalence of EDS is 1 in 2,500 to 1 in 5,000 people. It is a common but undiagnosed condition in India due to a lack of knowledge and training among medical and rehabilitation professionals. EDS is known to affect both males and females of all racial and ethnic backgrounds. There can be a wide or narrow range of severity within a family, but each person's case of EDS will be unique.

Early diagnosis of EDS is crucial to positive patient health outcomes. EDS are currently classified into 13 subtypes. A person's physical signs and symptoms will be matched up to the major and minor criteria to identify the subtype that is the most complete fit. While there is no cure for EDS, treatment is available to manage its symptoms, and preventive measures can be beneficial for most patients.

EDS/HSD is a multisystem disorder. EDS/HSD affects the body's connective tissues, which provide structure and support for all organs and tissues, including the skin, ligaments, bones, and blood vessels. When these connective tissues are defective, as they are in EDS, it results in widespread issues throughout the body. Examples include:

  • Musculoskeletal System: Weak connective tissues lead to joint hypermobility and instability, causing frequent dislocations and chronic pain.
  • Integumentary System (Skin): Skin is often stretchy (hyperextensible) and may be fragile, leading to poor wound healing and unusual scarring.
  • Cardiovascular System: Fragile blood vessels can lead to complications such as aneurysms, dissections, and ruptures of major or minor arteries, which can be life-threatening. Mitral Valve Prolapse and Varicose veins are often present.
  • Gastrointestinal System: Weakened connective tissue in the esophagus can cause Gastroesophageal Reflux, Hiatus Hernia, Gastroparesis, Maldigestion, and Malabsorption. Other gastrointestinal issues, such as chronic constipation, Irritable Bowel Syndrome, Abdominal Pain, and Dyspepsia, can also occur.
  • Neurological System: Many people with EDS experience neurological issues like headaches, fatigue, autonomic dysfunction (dysautonomia), and Postural Orthostatic Tachycardia Syndrome (POTS)
  • Immune System: Immune Dysregulation, Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome (CIRS), Mast Cell Activation Syndrome (MACS), Other Autoimmune Conditions, Gut Dysbiosis / Metabolic Endotoxemia.
  • Bioenergetics: Mitochondrial Dysfunction, Exercise Intolerance/weakness, Chronic Pain, Chronic Fatigue Syndrome, headache disorder, Dysautonomia,
  • Micro/Macro nutrient deficiencies, and Excessive Reactive Oxygen Species.
  • Other Organs and Systems: Problems with the eyes, teeth, and pelvic floor (including heavy periods and endometriosis in women) are also common.

Our Approach

  1. The RECOUP Hypermobility Rehabilitation Program (RHRP) offers an integrative, root-cause rehabilitation approach for EDS.
  2. Over two decades, RECOUP has developed considerable expertise in managing the most severe and complex end of the hypermobility spectrum, and offers a physician-led, intensive, comprehensive, holistic, evidence-based, interdisciplinary, protocol-based rehabilitation approach under one roof, using a combination of lifestyle medicine, functional medicine, physical medicine and neuromusculoskeletal rehabilitation, orthopedic, pain management, nutritional and psychological interventions.
  3. All patients are initially evaluated by Dr. Sharan, who establishes the diagnosis, orders necessary tests, prescribes medications, performs interventional pain procedures as needed, refers patients to other specialists, and develops a personalized rehabilitation plan. He reviews the patient on a weekly basis or more frequently if necessary.
  4. This approach involves various treatments that may last 4 to 5 hours daily for several weeks, with a team of medical and rehabilitation professionals meeting regularly to enhance communication and coordination of care. The patient actively participates in the rehabilitation process.
  5. The initial inpatient rehabilitation program is recommended for 4 weeks in most cases. Long-term follow-up is necessary, and further treatment may be recommended, either as an outpatient or inpatient, depending on the patient's progress. Ongoing monitoring and advice are available via email, WhatsApp, or online consultation.
  6. After completing the RHRP, patients are encouraged to continue using these techniques to further improve their independence and function.
  7. RHRP is not suitable for patients whose symptoms are primarily driven by other medical comorbidities to an extent that they cannot participate in an intensive neuromusculoskeletal rehabilitation program.

Aims of RRHP

  • Alleviate symptoms, such as pain and fatigue.
  • Improving physical function, e.g., activities of daily living
  • Identify and optimize physiological imbalances, e.g., gut dysbiosis, nutrient deficiencies
  • Minimize reliance on medication.
  • Lessen impairment and disability.
  • Long-term self-management strategies.
  • Prevent recurrence.

RECOUP's proprietary Assessment Tools

RHRP involves a personalized assessment of a patient's physical, mental, and emotional limitations and goals.

  1. RECOUP Entire Person Health Score and Care Plan Generation using AI: EPHS-S, EPHS-P, EPHS-O
  2. RECOUP PAIN PHENOTYPE SCORE: Patients are classified into different phenotypes and endotypes to characterize diseases better and select more precise therapeutic and management approaches. This helps predict adverse factors for chronicity and poor outcomes, allowing for tailored treatment approaches, such as pharmacological, physical therapy, psychologically informed rehabilitation, education, and lifestyle medicine.
  3. Virtual Ergonomic Workplace Analysis Tool.
  4. Objective physical assessments, such as the Wagner Pressure Algometer, Jamar Dynamometer, Instrumented Cervical ROM Device, Instrumented Gait Analysis, Instrumented Posture Analysis, Cardiometabolic Analyzer, Foot Pressure Analysis, Quantitative Sensory Testing, Balance Platform, and others, are available.

DEEPAK SHARAN'S Protocol for EDS/HSD

Machine learning clinical decision support for interdisciplinary multimodal rehabilitation

It is based on the following principles:

  • Disability and chronicity prevention
  • Early relief and reactivation
  • Enhanced lifestyle
  • Personalized, phenotypic, risk-stratified interventions
  • Address psychoneuroimmune factors and sensitization
  • Known co-morbidities treated
  • Structural restoration, if applicable
  • Heal physiological imbalances
  • Awareness of the body
  • Relapse prevention and management
  • Augment self-efficacy
  • Neuroplasticity promotion
  • Systems approach

RECOUP is guided by the philosophy that individuals can fully recover from chronic pain, even in advanced stages, by employing intensive, comprehensive, physician-directed, and sequential, integrative, root-cause, interdisciplinary rehabilitation strategies.

Phases of SHARAN'S® Protocol

Phase Description VAS Goals
Phase 1 (Relief) Severe discomfort > 7 1.Provide pain relief and reactivation,
2.Eliminate MTrP's, tenderness and muscle spasm,
3.Restore muscle flexibility.
4.Decrease inflammation and oxidative burden.
5.Improve sleep.
Phase 2 (Root-cause Reversal) Moderate discomfort 4-7 1.Reduce nerve tension and establish normal blood flow,
2.Correct joint dysfunction.
3.Promote inflammatory and antioxidant balance
4.Increase pain-free ROM and activity
5.Stress reduction
6.Treat anxiety, depression, emotional trauma
7.Identify and correct macro- and micro-nutrient deficiencies
Phase 3 (Rebalance) Mild discomfort 1-3 1.Postural correction,
2.Strengthen muscles so that they do not fall back on nerves and blood vessels again,
3.Prepare for return to work
4.Heal gut, hormonal balance (HPA Axis, Thyroid).
5.Blood sugar balance.
6.Promote neuroplasticity
Phase 4 (Regeneration) Maintenance < 1 1.Improve strength and endurance,
2.Reduce the risk of recurrence
3.Weight management

RECOUP is the only center in the world that has professionals with expertise, experience, training, and qualifications to deliver this protocol.

Phase 1: Pain relief and reactivation: (Pain Scale > 7/10) Treatment: 5-6 hours/day

  • Therapeutic Neuroscience Education
  • Self-management to learn more about the disease process, recognizing and preventing 'flare-ups'; flare-up plan, pacing strategies, and other problem-solving and coping skills to improve quality of life, reduce visits to health care providers, and decrease associated costs.
  • Functional and lifestyle medicine assessment and interventions
  • Medication for neuropathic pain to allow for improved activity level.
  • Motivational Interviewing
  • Narrative Based Medicine, Conversations inviting change
  • Establishing a therapeutic alliance
  • Psychological flexibility
  • Mindfulness Training
  • Positional Release Techniques
  • Dermoneuromodulation
  • Niromathe Method
  • Quantitative Somatosensory Testing and Rehabilitation
  • Nimmo Receptor Tonus Method
  • Primal Reflex Release Technique
  • Bowen's Technique
  • Fascial Therapy
  • Neurofascial mobilization
  • Myofascial Release
  • Muscle Energy Technique
  • Myofascial Induction Therapy
  • Active Fascial Release
  • Danis Bois Method Fasciatherapy
  • Myofascial Manipulation
  • Dorn Method
  • Spray and stretch (Ethyl Chloride or Ice Lolly)
  • Trigger Point injections or Dry Needling
  • Physiological pain portal blocks
  • Ozone and prolozone therapy, Extracorporeal Blood Oxygenation and Ozonation (EBOO)
  • Far and Near Infrared Therapy
  • Molecular Hydrogen Therapy
  • Aquatic Therapy in a temperature-controlled swimming pool
  • Integrated Neuromuscular Inhibition Technique
  • Low-Level Laser Therapy
  • Pulsed Electro Magnetic Field Therapy
  • Kinesiology, Biomechanical or Functional Taping
  • Relaxation techniques
  • Vagus nerve stimulation
  • Frequency Specific Microcurrent
  • Breathwork
  • Augmented Reality Based Therapy (Relaxation, Graded exposure, Desensitization)
  • Sleep assessment and interventions
  • Prolotherapy and Perineural Injections
  • Platelet Rich Plasma
  • Interventional Pain Management Techniques
  • Rehabilitation of headaches, orofacial, or abdominopelvic pain
  • Nutrition assessment and interventions
  • Limbic system rehabilitation
  • Assessment of bone health
  • Orthotic management to help protect and support joints, prevent or correct joint deformities, and improve the ability to perform activities safely with less pain. Includes Compressive Clothing and Customized Textured Shoe Inserts

Phase 2: Restoration of Activity, Joint Stabilization and Postural training (Pain Scale 4-7/10) Treatment (2-3 hours/day)

  • Fascial Manipulation (Stecco method)
  • Fascial Distortion Model
  • Fascial and membrane techniques applied to superficial fascia, deep fascia, septa, retinacula, and interosseous membrane
  • Structural Integration
  • Vitality Flossing
  • Neural Mobilization
  • Visceromyofascial techniques
  • Lymphatic drainage, Vibrassage
  • Progressive stress loading
  • Neuromuscular, Kinesiology, or Functional Taping
  • Aquatic therapy: Proprioceptive Neuromuscular Facilitation (PNF), mobilization, and strengthening in water
  • Functional Electrical Stimulation with progressive exercise
  • Instrument-assisted soft tissue mobilization using FAKTR (Functional and Kinetic Treatment with Rehabilitation, Provocation, and Motion) concepts
  • Supervised, individualized graded exercise therapy, including range of motion exercises, PNF patterns, and progressive resistance training (e.g., Muldowney Protocol, Derby Protocol for Shoulder)
  • Energy conservation programs and precautions to safely strengthen muscles, protect joints, and prevent further damage
  • Occupational therapy to improve daily activities such as dressing, grooming, eating, and household chores
  • Assessment and rehabilitation of postural dysfunction using Janda, Dynamic Neuromuscular Stabilization (Kolar), and the Gokhale method
  • Assessment and rehabilitation of Breathing Pattern Dysfunction
  • Rehabilitation of Postural Orthostatic Tachycardia Syndrome
  • Vestibular rehabilitation
  • Gait training and postural stability improvement
  • Virtual Reality-Based Therapy
  • EMG Biofeedback, Somatic Feedback, Neurofeedback, Autogenic Training
  • Yoga
  • Specific assessment and rehabilitation for performing artists or athletes
  • Body mechanics training and ergonomic accessory recommendations

Phase 3: Strengthening and aerobic conditioning (Pain Scale 2-3/10) Treatment (45 min /day)

  • Further progression of strengthening and proprioception exercises (Muldowney Protocol, Derby Protocol for Shoulder, etc.)
  • Movement re-education
  • Dynamic Neurocognitive Imagery (Franklin method)
  • Aerobic conditioning
  • Fascial Fitness
  • Supervised work training on the model workstation
  • Strengthening using EMG Biofeedback
  • Yoga
  • Evolution of Movement
  • Alexander Technique
  • Feldenkrais

Phase 4: Functional restoration (Improve strength and endurance) (Pain Scale < 2/10) Treatment (45 min/week)

  • Further Aerobic Conditioning and Strength Training
  • Taìjíquán (Zheng Manqing Style)
  • Qi Gong
  • Core Stabilization and Sensory Motor Training
  • Functional restoration using the concepts of FAKTR

About RECOUP

  • RECOUP is an internationally recognized center of excellence, specializing in the prevention, assessment, treatment, and research of EDS/HSD, Chronic Pain, Fibromyalgia Syndrome, Chronic Fatigue Syndrome, Arthritis, Neuromusculoskeletal Disorders, and Locomotor Disabilities.
  • RECOUP provides personalized care emphasizing patient satisfaction. Patients report sustained recovery due to careful attention to underlying factors (e.g., catastrophizing, pain-related fear, stress, habitual deep muscle tension, nutrition, biomechanical issues, and medical comorbidities, such as metabolic bone disease), rather than just symptom relief.
  • RECOUP offers one of the two specialized and structured inpatient rehabilitation programs for EDS/HSD internationally – the other being at the Royal National Orthopaedic Hospital in Stanmore, UK (which focuses only on physical and psychological rehabilitation).

About the head of the EDS/HSD Rehabilitation Program

Dr. Deepak Sharan
Consultant in Orthopedic Surgery, Rehabilitation, Musculoskeletal and Pain Medicine, Ergonomics, Occupational Health, Functional, Lifestyle, Precision and Anti-Aging Medicine, Founder and CEO, RECOUP, Bengaluru, India. With over 34 years of clinical experience in India and the UK, including more than 25 years focused on managing EDS/HSD. President of the International Myopain Society and Founder President of the Indian Myopain Society. Served as Organizing Chairperson for MYOPAIN 2017: The 10th World Congress on Myofascial Pain Syndrome and Fibromyalgia Syndrome held in Bengaluru. Founder Director of EPM International Ergonomics School in Milan, Italy (www.epmresearch.org) and President of the Indian Ergonomics School. Senior Faculty at Myopain Seminars, USA, for Dry Needling and Myofascial Pain Syndrome (www.myopainseminars.com), and Director of Myopain Seminars, India. In 2001, he was the first to describe the role of EDS/HSD as a risk factor in developing work-related musculoskeletal disorders such as Thoracic Outlet Syndrome and multidirectional shoulder instability in IT professionals, publishing this association in the peer-reviewed international journal "Work" in 2012, and presenting it at multiple World Congresses on Pain, Ergonomics, and Occupational Health. Has authored over 600 scientific publications and conference presentations and has received numerous top international research awards and grants. He is a certified Postural Orthostatic Tachycardia Syndrome (POTS) Provider (Emily Rich).

Special training of RECOUP's staff in EDS/HSD

The first EDS India Conference was held at RECOUP in Bengaluru in 2017, featuring leading figures in international EDS research, as well as authors of nearly all landmark textbooks and papers on the topic. Dr. Deepak Sharan led a two-day workshop on the Rehabilitation of EDS/HSD during the course titled "New Concepts in the Management of Complex Pain" at RECOUP, Bengaluru, in May 2019. RECOUP maintains close ties with the Ehlers-Danlos Society (www.ehlers-danlos.com) and several of its medical experts, supporting ongoing collaborative research and continuous education for both professionals and patients.

RECOUP's staff has received exclusive and personalized hands-on training in EDS/HSD and related rehabilitation.

Testimonials

I have been pleasantly surprised by the skill level and knowledge of the therapists—absolutely world-class. Not only were the skills, experience, and knowledge of the individual therapists excellent, but their approach was also commendable. A very positive aspect of their strategy was their ability to understand the 'patient's' perspective and the patient's need to solve the problem. Everyone was able to co-think with me in a genuine quest to resolve my issues, session after session, with no attempt to 'talk away' the problem—something I had experienced many times here in Holland. Even when I tried to talk away the problem myself, the therapist corrected me. This truly demonstrates their motivation to help, which is a very powerful attitude and extremely helpful for someone dealing with a chronic pain condition. Whenever I felt frustrated with my progress, I found a listening ear, and together we tried to identify the primary source of the root problem. My advice and feedback here—do not change a thing... The reception staff was very friendly and incredibly helpful. They occasionally made my day brighter. The pickup and introduction process was well organized. I found the rooms to be good; everything was perfectly functional. The cooks provided amazing meals. The incredible rice and diverse dishes really contributed to a pleasant stay, and I believe one cannot underestimate the importance of good food. (Kuno, Netherlands)

The program at RECOUP has several strengths: 1. The intensive treatment schedule with multiple sessions each day. 2. The interdisciplinary approach to care. Myotherapy forms the core of the treatment, but a crucial addition includes yoga therapy with stress management that emphasizes proper breathing, along with cognitive-behavioral therapy and fitness sessions. There are also sessions on ergonomics and nutrition. Since musculoskeletal issues are often complex, this integrated approach helps patients sustainably address the root causes. Lastly, the therapists and staff are exceptionally skilled, warm, and enthusiastic. I have never encountered an organization filled with so much positive energy. (Ingela, Norway)

Monthly cost of Inpatient rehabilitation

  • Approximately INR 50000 for consultations and rehabilitation by in-house experts. Accommodation, airport transfers, and food on actuals (approximately INR 50000 per month). Medicines, supplements, specialized interventions (e.g., injections, intravenous therapy), and Investigations (e.g., blood tests, MRI scans, Ultrasound Scans, Neurophysiology studies, bone mineral density tests) are charged extra, and the costs can range from INR 5,000 to INR 25,000.
  • To confirm the booking, a non-refundable payment of INR 25,000 by bank transfer is required. Further payment can be made on arrival by credit card (Visa/Mastercard).
